text.title: predictive-analytics-pm Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for predictive-analytics-pm as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Survivorship bias: killed projects are excluded, making the training set systematically optimistic about completion outcomes.

failure-mode.description: Shipping a model without a drift monitor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Organisational change, new domains, and new tooling break models trained on prior projects within 6-18 months. Silent failure ensues.

failure-mode.description: LLM "predicts" budget overruns from prose narrative without running a model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are not numeric forecasters. They confabulate plausible-sounding numbers. Train a model; LLMs draft, explain, and orchestrate.

failure-mode.description: Random train/test split used for project data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Random splits leak future project data into past training. Group splits by project_id or program are mandatory.

failure-mode.description: Agent declares "deployed" after model.fit() without wiring a drift monitor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shipping a model without monitoring is silent failure. Force a monitoring task into the SDD chain before calling any deployment complete.

failure-mode.description: Sending raw PM CSV rows to a third-party LLM API.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PM data contains salary, vendor pricing, customer names. Tokenize or run on-prem; never send raw rows externally.
